page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m16-vws.5xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration provides 20 physical cores with 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 92.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.6 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and parallel computing workloads are accelerated by a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, requiring external block storage solutions. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1105.39 and a poor gzip single-threaded compression rate of 19.26 GB/sec. However, multi-core capabilities are more robust, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14732.17 and a Passmark CPU score of 15713.85, both falling into the average tier. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 822.1 GB/sec, the overall Passmark memory score is weak. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform at average levels, with Redis SET operations achieving 14.5 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads without the premium cost of high-end training GPUs. The inclusion of the NVIDIA T4 GPU makes it suitable for virtual workstations, video transcoding, and low-latency inference tasks. Given the average multi-core performance and lack of local storage, it is well-suited for cloud-based rendering, 3D visualization, and moderately demanding parallel computing tasks rather than high-performance storage-heavy databases or single-threaded legacy applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m16-vws.5xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 92.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate weak single-core CPU and memory performance, but average multi-core CPU capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 15713.85 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 14732.17. This hardware profile provides a cost-effective option for GPU-accelerated workloads. It is best suited for virtual workstations, video transcoding, 3D rendering, and moderate parallel computing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m16-vws.5xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 92 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM, designed for virtual workstations and rendering.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.vgn6i-m16-vws.5xlarge features 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 92 GB RAM, and one NVIDIA T4 GPU for virtual workstation workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and an NVIDIA T4 GPU for parallel computing and virtual workstations.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
1x NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM
92.0 GB of system memory (4.6 GB per core)
No local storage included (requires external block storage)
Optimized for virtual workstations, 3D rendering, and video transcoding
